annotations.label: The number 4

anchor.snippet: It is the natural number following 3 and preceding 5

annotations.note: The natural number 4 exists via numeral literals but there is no dedicated declaration formalizing 4 as a mathematical object.

annotations.label: Lagrange's four-square theorem

anchor.snippet: every positive integer can be written as the sum of at most four squares

annotations.note: Nat.sum_four_squares states every natural n equals a²+b²+c²+d² for some naturals, exactly Lagrange's four-square theorem.

annotations.label: Four is an all-Harshad number

anchor.snippet: Four is one of four all-Harshad numbers

annotations.note: Harshad/Niven numbers are not defined anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiples of 4 as difference of squares

anchor.snippet: Each natural number divisible by 4 is a difference of squares of two natural numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has the algebraic identity sq_sub_sq but not the number-theoretic statement that every multiple of 4 is a difference of two natural squares.

annotations.label: Quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A four-sided plane figure is a quadrilateral or quadrangle

annotations.note: There is no dedicated quadrilateral definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solvability of degree-4 polynomials by radicals

anchor.snippet: Four is the highest degree general polynomial equation for which there is a solution in radicals

annotations.note: Mathlib defines solvableByRad and proves the negative Abel–Ruffini direction (a quintic root not solvable by radicals), but does not formalize that all degree-≤4 generals are solvable by radicals.

annotations.label: Four as the only square one more than a prime

anchor.snippet: Four is the only square number

annotations.note: This specific arithmetic fact about 4 is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four-color theorem

anchor.snippet: The four-color theorem states that a planar graph

annotations.note: Mathlib has graph coloring infrastructure but neither planarity nor the four-color theorem is formalized.

annotations.label: Largest planar complete graph

anchor.snippet: The largest planar complete graph has four vertices

annotations.note: Planarity of graphs is not defined in Mathlib (Kuratowski here refers only to metric-space embeddings).

annotations.label: Tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: A solid figure with four faces as well as four vertices is a tetrahedron

annotations.note: A tetrahedron is the n=3 case of Affine.Simplex (n+1 affinely independent points), but there is no dedicated tetrahedron definition.

annotations.label: Regular tetrahedron as simplest Platonic solid

anchor.snippet: The regular tetrahedron, also called a 3- simplex , is the simplest Platonic solid

annotations.note: The 3-simplex is captured by Affine.Simplex k P 3, but regularity and the notion of Platonic solid are not formalized.

annotations.label: Klein four-group

anchor.snippet: The smallest non- cyclic group has four elements; it is the Klein four-group

annotations.note: IsKleinFour is a mixin class characterizing the Klein four-group as a group of cardinality four and exponent two.

annotations.label: Non-simplicity of small alternating groups

anchor.snippet: alternating groups are not simple for values

annotations.note: Mathlib proves alternatingGroup α is simple when 5 ≤ card α, but does not state the complementary non-simplicity for the small (≤4) cases.

annotations.label: Four Hopf fibrations of hyperspheres

anchor.snippet: There are four Hopf fibrations of hyperspheres

annotations.note: Hopf fibrations are not in Mathlib (the 'Hopf' files concern Hopf algebras, an unrelated topic).

annotations.label: Hopf fibrations as locally trivial fibrations

anchor.snippet: They are defined as locally trivial fibrations that map

annotations.note: Hopf fibrations are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Knuth's up-arrow notation
annotations.note: Knuth's up-arrow / hyperoperation notation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four dimensions of Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: There are four dimensions in the theory of Minkowski space

annotations.note: Minkowski spacetime is not formalized in Mathlib (Minkowski references there concern convex-geometry theorems).
